seo.title: Building Content Generation with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service content generation

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables powerful LLM-driven content generation workflows with built-in Azure OpenAI integration, grounding capabilities, and enterprise security. However, without proper guardrails, agents can produce brand-inconsistent content, factually inaccurate material, or violate editorial guidelines. Vex provides essential middleware to enforce brand voice, fact-checking, and compliance verification in your content pipelines.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with user input routed through Azure AI Agent Service's agent threads, which orchestrate Azure OpenAI models for content drafting. Agents leverage built-in tools like Bing Search for grounding and Azure AI Search for RAG-based fact retrieval, generating initial drafts that pass through optional human-in-the-loop review.

The pipeline then applies post-generation processing for brand voice alignment and SEO optimization before final publication. Vex integrates as middleware between agent execution and content output, validating against enterprise policies without disrupting the native Azure workflow.

Key components include agent threads for state management, tool calling for external data retrieval, and streaming responses for real-time content generation.

risks.name: Brand voice drift
risks.description: Generated content deviates from established brand tone, vocabulary, and style guidelines
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks against brand voice corpus

risks.name: Factual inaccuracies from Bing grounding
risks.description: Outdated or incorrect search results propagate hallucinations despite grounding
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-verifies facts against authoritative enterprise sources

risks.name: Token limit exhaustion mid-generation
risks.description: Long-form content exceeds agent thread context windows, causing incomplete outputs
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and triggers graceful summarization

risks.name: SEO compliance violations
risks.description: Content fails keyword density, readability scores, or technical SEO requirements
risks.mitigation: Vex automated SEO scoring and optimization suggestions

implementation.title: Initialize Azure AI Agent Project
implementation.description: Set up the agent service client and create a thread for content generation workflow
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AIProjectsClient
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
client = AIProjectsClient(
    endpoint="https://your-project.openai.azure.com/",
    credential=credential,
    api_version="2024-10-21"
)

# Create agent thread
thread = client.threads.create(
    messages=[
        {
            "role": "user",
            "content": "Write a 800-word blog post about AI ethics in enterprise"
        }
    ]
)
print(f"Thread created: {thread.id}")

implementation.title: Run Content Generation Agent
implementation.description: Execute the agent with grounding tools and stream the generated content
implementation.code:
run = client.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id="your-content-agent-id",
    instructions="""Generate content following:
    - Brand voice: Professional, optimistic, enterprise-focused
    - Length: 800 words
    - Include SEO keywords: AI ethics, enterprise AI, responsible AI
    - Cite sources from Bing grounding"""
)

# Stream run events
stream = client.runs.stream(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id)
for event in stream:
    if event.type == "thread.message.delta":
        print(event.data.content[0].text.value, end='')
